message:
refactor(excerpt-builder): rewrite parseExcerpt for multi-line quote support

The previous regex-based implementation of parseExcerpt() failed to
correctly parse Brenner transcript excerpts that spanned multiple lines.
Quotes from the transcript often wrap across several lines in the
markdown blockquote format, but the regex approach only captured content
on the same line as the anchor.

Problem example that failed:
> **§42**: "The important thing about doing experiments is
> not just to get positive results. It's to find out what's
> really going on."
> — *On Experimental Design*

The old implementation would only capture "The important thing about
doing experiments is not just to get positive results." and miss the
rest of the quote.

Solution:
Replaced the regex-based approach with a line-by-line state machine:

1. Iterate through each line of the markdown
2. Detect quote block starts: > **§n**: "text...
3. Accumulate continuation lines: > more text...
4. Detect attributions: > — *Title*
5. Push completed sections when a new quote starts or EOF

New helper function cleanQuote():
- Joins accumulated lines with spaces
- Strips surrounding quotation marks
- Trims whitespace

This ensures that multi-line transcript excerpts from the Brenner
corpus are correctly parsed and displayed in the Research Brief and
Evidence Summary components, preserving the full context of each quote.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
